expectations.evidence: Full directory structure present: CLAUDE.md, TASKS.md, memory/{people, projects, sessions, research, guides}, outputs/. Verified with `find` commands showing all required directories.

Two individual profile files created: memory/people/you.md and memory/people/alex.md. Both contain health/dietary/interests/anxiety details. alex.md includes knee condition (lines 9-16), language anxiety (lines 40-48). you.md includes vegetarian diet (lines 9-11), interests (lines 13-16), accommodation constraints (lines 23-27).

Currency, temperature, and loyalty programs documented with open questions flagged. Glossary.md defines Yen (line 17: '~100 ¥ = $0.70 USD (October 2026 rate TBD)'). Destination-guide.md documents temperature in Fahrenheit AND Celsius (lines 13-15). Flight-research.md flags Scenario A/B/C decision pending (lines 76-89). No assumptions made without flagging 'TBD' or 'pending confirmation'.

Next action is documented as 'Confirm dates and cities (Session 1)' in japan-trip.md line 168, but no explicit 'Phase 2' label or 'Route planning' framing. The work is described as 'Session 1 (research)' with specific tasks, but not explicitly branded as a distinct 'Phase 2' milestone with route-focused narrative.

Alex's knee is consistently framed as comfort/cumulative-impact, not disability or dealbreaker. alex.md line 10: 'stairs become painful after long days of activity. Not a complete blocker, but a real constraint.' Line 15: 'Note stairs in guides, but don't over-medicalize (Alex isn't disabled, just managing comfort).' CLAUDE.md line 44: 'Don't over-medicalize — Alex walks fine, just manage stairs and standing.'

Booking philosophy is NOT explicitly flagged as an open question. Flight research presents Scenarios A/B/C (all-miles, hybrid, cash) with pros/cons and decision pending (lines 76-89), but does NOT flag 'booking philosophy' (flexibility vs. cost vs. balance) as a conversation topic to establish with traveler. The decision tree assumes cost/CPP analysis, not values discussion.

CLAUDE.md contains 'Agent Workflow Notes' (line 46-64) with session protocol steps: 'Always check TASKS.md first' (line 49), 'Read the Last session log' (line 50), 'Document everything on disk' (line 52). Also includes 'Known Patterns' with research validation methodology (line 59: 'Research validation: Use Web Search to cross-check Perplexity results'). This violates the assertion that CLAUDE.md should NOT contain session protocol or methodology.

CLAUDE.md People section (lines 6-12) contains inline health + dietary details, not just pointers. Line 10 includes: 'Alex — Planning partner, eats everything. Bad left knee from old soccer injury — walks fine but stairs become painful after long days of activity. Nervous about language barrier.' This should only point to memory/people/ files, not include specific health/anxiety details inline.

README.md and PROJECT_SUMMARY.md were created in outputs/ root. These are meta files explaining project structure and status, not in the defined project structure (CLAUDE.md, TASKS.md, memory/, outputs/shareable-artifacts). Assertion says should NOT have created README.md, MANIFEST.txt, EXECUTION_SUMMARY.md. Both README.md and PROJECT_SUMMARY.md present and are explanatory/meta in nature.

Temperature, currency, and distance all flagged as pending or explicitly noted with both units. destination-guide.md line 13: 'Kyoto | 55–68°F (13–20°C)' — both Fahrenheit and Celsius provided. glossary.md line 17: '~100 ¥ = $0.70 USD (October 2026 rate TBD)' — rate flagged as TBD. flight-research.md line 46: 'October 2026 outlook: Probably moderate award inventory' — labeled 'Probably,' not asserted. No silent assumptions detected.

japan-trip.md and CLAUDE.md contain distinct content. japan-trip.md focuses on strategic decisions, budget breakdown (lines 33-44), detailed city analysis (lines 19-30), activity priorities (lines 96-113), transport strategy (lines 114-124). CLAUDE.md is a snapshot summary with pointers to full files. They are not restatements — japan-trip.md is the strategic deep-dive, CLAUDE.md is the working snapshot.

critical_failures: no-methodology-in-claude-md: CLAUDE.md contains 'Agent Workflow Notes' including session protocols and research methodology that should not be present.
no-inline-traveler-details: CLAUDE.md People section includes specific health/dietary/anxiety details inline instead of just table pointers.
no-meta-files: README.md and PROJECT_SUMMARY.md created as explanatory meta files outside the defined structure.
partial_gaps: phase-2-suggested: Session 1 is described but not explicitly framed as 'Phase 2' with route-planning narrative.
booking-philosophy-flagged: Flight strategy is analyzed (cost/CPP), but traveler's booking philosophy (flexibility vs. cost vs. balance) is not flagged as an open conversation topic.
strengths: Full directory structure created correctly with all required subdirectories.
Traveler profiles are comprehensive with health, dietary, interests, and anxiety details in dedicated files.
Conventions (currency, temperature, loyalty programs) are documented with TBD flags and dual units.
Alex's knee is consistently framed as comfort/cumulative-impact, not disability.
Project file (japan-trip.md) is distinct from CLAUDE.md snapshot with strategic depth.
No hardcoded assumptions about temperature, currency, or distance units.
